root_text.sn47.20:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sumbhesu viharati sedakaṁ nāma sumbhānaṁ nigamo.
root_text.sn47.20:2.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, ‘janapadakalyāṇī, janapadakalyāṇī’ti kho, bhikkhave, mahājanakāyo sannipateyya.
root_text.sn47.20:2.2: ‘Sā kho panassa janapadakalyāṇī paramapāsāvinī nacce, paramapāsāvinī gīte.
root_text.sn47.20:2.3: Janapadakalyāṇī naccati gāyatī’ti kho, bhikkhave, bhiyyoso mattāya mahājanakāyo sannipateyya.
root_text.sn47.20:2.4: Atha puriso āgaccheyya jīvitukāmo amaritukāmo sukhakāmo dukkhappaṭikūlo.
root_text.sn47.20:2.6: ‘ayaṁ te, ambho purisa, samatittiko telapatto antarena ca mahāsamajjaṁ antarena ca janapadakalyāṇiyā pariharitabbo.
root_text.sn47.20:2.7: Puriso ca te ukkhittāsiko piṭṭhito piṭṭhito anubandhissati.
root_text.sn47.20:2.8: Yattheva naṁ thokampi chaḍḍessati tattheva te siro pātessatī’ti.
root_text.sn47.20:2.10: api nu so puriso amuṁ telapattaṁ amanasikaritvā bahiddhā pamādaṁ āhareyyā”ti?
root_text.sn47.20:3.1: “Upamā kho myāyaṁ, bhikkhave, katā atthassa viññāpanāya.
root_text.sn47.20:3.3: samatittiko telapattoti kho, bhikkhave, kāyagatāya etaṁ satiyā adhivacanaṁ.
root_text.sn47.20:3.5: ‘kāyagatā sati no bhāvitā bhavissati bahulīkatā yānīkatā vatthukatā anuṭṭhitā paricitā susamāraddhā’ti.

translation_text.sn47.20:0.3: The Finest Lady in the Land
translation_text.sn47.20:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Sumbhas, near the town of the Sumbhas named Sedaka.
translation_text.sn47.20:1.3: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.sn47.20:2.1: “Mendicants, suppose that on hearing, ‘The finest lady in the land! The finest lady in the land!’ a large crowd would gather.
translation_text.sn47.20:2.2: And the finest lady in the land would dance and sing in a most thrilling way.
translation_text.sn47.20:2.3: On hearing, ‘The finest lady in the land is dancing and singing! The finest lady in the land is dancing and singing!’ an even larger crowd would gather.
translation_text.sn47.20:2.4: Then a person would come along who wants to live and doesn’t want to die, who wants to be happy and recoils from pain.
translation_text.sn47.20:2.6: ‘My friend, this is a bowl full to the brim with oil. You must carry it in between this large crowd and the finest lady in the land.
translation_text.sn47.20:2.7: And a man with a drawn sword will follow right behind you.
translation_text.sn47.20:2.8: Wherever you spill even a drop, he’ll chop off your head right there.’
translation_text.sn47.20:2.10: Would that person lose focus on that bowl, and negligently get distracted outside?”
translation_text.sn47.20:3.1: “I’ve made up this simile to make a point.
translation_text.sn47.20:3.2: And this is what it means.
translation_text.sn47.20:3.3: ‘A bowl of oil filled to the brim’ is a term for mindfulness of the body.
translation_text.sn47.20:3.4: So you should train like this:
translation_text.sn47.20:3.5: ‘We will develop mindfulness of the body. We’ll cultivate it, make it our vehicle and our basis, keep it up, consolidate it, and properly implement it.’
